commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(failover): classify 'budget exceeded' proxy errors as billing
target.body:
## Summary

Problem: OpenAI-compatible proxies return "Budget has been exceeded" on quota/budget overruns, but this message isn't in the billing pattern list, so it gets misclassified as a format error (on HTTP 400) or a generic rate limit (on HTTP 429).

Why it matters: Users see vague "something went wrong" or "rate limited, try again" messages and sit through unnecessary schema retries and model/provider fallbacks that can't possibly resolve a budget overrun, wasting time and burning through backup credentials.

What changed: Added a single case-insensitive "budget has been exceeded" pattern to ERROR_PATTERNS.billing in src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/failover-matches.ts, plus targeted test assertions covering isBillingErrorMessage and classifyFailoverReason for the new text on both HTTP 400 and429 paths.

What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to classification engine signatures, HTTP status routing, billing downstream handling, public API/config/CLI surface, other error categories (auth/rateLimit/overloaded/timeout), or any unrelated pattern — strictly one pattern added to an existing list.

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- [ ] Chore/infra
